question_text: Two waves have the same frequency but different wavelengths. What can you conclude about their speeds?

options.label: A
options.text: They must have the same speed because frequency is constant

options.label: B
options.text: They have different speeds because the wave equation shows speed depends on both frequency and wavelength

options.label: C
options.text: We cannot determine their speeds without knowing the medium

options.label: D
options.text: The wave with the shorter wavelength travels faster

explanation: Using the wave equation (speed = frequency × wavelength), if frequency is the same but wavelengths differ, the speeds must be different. For example, if one wave has twice the wavelength, it must have twice the speed. The medium affects both waves equally, so option C is incorrect.
